title: Chunked Transfer Coding, Extensions, Trailers, and Decoding · Language Lens: Chunk Data Boundary
summary: A decoder parses chunk sizes as decimal, trusts extensions, misses overflow, forgets the zero chunk, merges forbidden trailers into headers, and leaves bytes misaligned. Planned artifact: Add and verify the chunk data boundary increment in incremental chunked decoder and trailer policy. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Read exactly each chunk-data length plus its terminating CRLF across arbitrary fragmentation.
Parse bounded hexadecimal chunk size with extensions, overflow checks, exact CRLF, and cumulative decoded limits.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Chunk Data Boundary · Theory Chunk Data Boundary
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory protocol team handles case 3517 in a protocol upgrade gateway transferring buffered tunnel bytes. Build deterministic Go evidence to bound chunk accumulation and require trailer validation after the zero chunk; preserve this operating constraint: no malformed framing may be normalized and forwarded to another recipient; then inject Transfer-Encoding plus Content-Length and close before any downstream forwarding. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Chunk Data Boundary contract for the supplied data.

Evidence target: Predict Chunk Data Boundary · Theory Chunk Data Boundary.
